candidates.sentence: This opening names the system as a stack, the efficiency layer explains why that stack can be efficient, the authority layer prevents efficiency from becoming permission leakage, the failure layer names what breaks without those boundaries, and the evidence layer governs how any of those claims may later move.
candidates.disposition_rationale: Reader-path summary of already owned chapter and downstream claims; it adds no independently testable proposition.

candidates.sentence: It is a governed system whose parts must say what they own, what they may do, what evidence they create, and how they can be stopped or replaced.
candidates.disposition_rationale: Restates the governed-stack core and layer-contract mechanism.

candidates.sentence: A powerful part must remain inspectable from outside itself.
candidates.disposition_rationale: Adds a distinct external-observability requirement for high-impact components.

candidates.sentence: When a later mechanism cannot name its responsibility, artifact, authority ceiling, evidence state, and failure mode, the right response is not enthusiasm.
candidates.disposition_rationale: Restates the mandatory Layer Boundary Record fields.

candidates.sentence: Advanced AI systems that plan, remember, verify, act, route work, compress representations, and improve under governance need one architecture frame.
candidates.disposition_rationale: Restates the manifest problem proposition.

candidates.sentence: An ASI system should not be described only by the model that generates tokens, the agent loop that calls tools, or the benchmark score that reports progress.
candidates.disposition_rationale: Restates the core contrast with model-, loop-, and benchmark-only descriptions.

candidates.sentence: If it cannot answer those questions, it may still be an idea, but it is not yet an architectural layer.

candidates.sentence: A retrieval system can surface a source without proving that the source is current, permitted, or sufficient for the task.
